g.text: The Peripheral System introduces functions outside the playable stages. These functions enhance
the player experience beyond core gameplay and provide progression, competition, and rewards.
Leaderboard | Rank (Competitive Ranking) | Achievements | Stage Settlement | Wonderland Gift Box
Records in-game information to the peripheral system. Players can view leaderboard data when
browsing stage information from the lobby.
Choose to enable either Leaderboard or Competitive Rank function (mutually exclusive).
Enable In-Room Leaderboard Ranking: Allow leaderboard data in room teams.
Custom name for the creator to distinguish
Identifier for modifying data via Node Graph
Higher priority = displayed closer to top
Do Not Reset, Reset With Season
Lower Values First, Higher Values First
Set Player Leaderboard Score as Float / Set Player Leaderboard Score as Integer
Defines player rank points within a stage. During stage settlement, players receive different rank
points based on settlement status (Victory/Defeat/Undefined/Escape). Points are customizable.
Rank information is visible to other players browsing the stage in the main hall.
Rank points are modified through preset score group templates. Each player uses one template.
Custom name for the creator to distinguish
Which players this score group applies to
Fleeing Penalties, Win Streak bonuses, Consecutive Escape penalties, Rank Protection
Switch Score Group | Set Player Rank Score Change | Get Player Rank Score Change | Get Player Rank Info
Records specific goals or milestones that players accomplish. Achievement progress carries over
across multiple playthroughs. Number and conditions are fully customizable by the creator.
Auto-unlocked when all normal achievements are completed.
Only enabled when 5+ normal achievements are set with names and images.
Target value; achievement completes when reached
Enable in-room achievement settlement: Allow progress in room teams.
Set Achievement Progress Tally | Change Achievement Progress Tally | Query If Achievement Is Completed
An action triggered from the Node Graph. Once triggered, the entire stage ends and the settlement
result is passed to the Peripheral System to update Leaderboard and Rank data.
A player attribute consisting of two fields:
False = player state set to "Escape State"
Victory / Defeat / Undefined (meaningful when Escape = True)
Configured in Stage Settings → Stage Settlement Settings
Use Ranking Settings node to edit display order
Low to High or High to Low
Settle Stage | Set Player Settlement Ranking Value | Set Faction Settlement Ranking Value
Set Player Settlement Success Status | Set Faction Settlement Success Status
Creates additional in-game content for distribution to players. Each stage can contain up to 20 Gift Boxes.
Access: System Menu → [Manage Wonderland Gift Box]
Unique identifier; must be unique across the stage
Choose from provided icons or upload custom icon
Indicates the quality of the Gift Box
Leaderboard: Choose leaderboard data + required rank
Ultimate Achievement: No additional editing needed
Can be obtained again after season update?
Enable = players can obtain again when Season updates
Disabled = permanent. Enabled = set season count
Description Content: Describes the Gift Box's function
Activate: When disabled, Gift Box unavailable
Display Priority: Higher values appear first in Shop
Permanent Effect After Activation: Stay listed permanently
Select Season → Add Gift Boxes to list for that Season
Consume Gift Box | Query Gift Box Quantity | Query Gift Box Consumption Quantity
1. Creator configures Leaderboard/Rank/Achievements/Gift Boxes in Peripheral System Settings
2. During gameplay, Node Graphs update player data (scores, rank points, achievement progress)
3. Stage Settlement is triggered → data passed to Peripheral System → Leaderboard and Rank updated
4. Gift Boxes are unlocked based on Leaderboard/Rank/Achievement conditions and can be consumed

svg.text: Generated by AI based on official Miliastra Academy documentation. Version: Luminary
